name: Simple state overrides — Florida baseline
description: Tests that state-level overrides (specificity 1) apply correctly. No client or investor overrides in play.

expectedResolutions.explanation: FL+Nationstar override (specificity 2) — Nationstar has a tighter title search deadline in FL
FL state override (specificity 1)
FL state override adds verification_of_complaint
FL 90-day service window (specificity 1)
No fee auth override for Nationstar — Chase-specific override (ovr-030) does not apply
FL-specific sale report template (specificity 1)

name: State + client stacking — Florida Chase
description: Tests that specificity-2 overrides (state+client) beat specificity-1 overrides (state-only).

expectedResolutions.explanation: FL+Chase (specificity 2) beats FL-only ovr-001 (specificity 1). 10-day deadline vs 15-day.
FL+Chase negotiated filing fee (specificity 2)
Chase global fee auth policy (specificity 1) — no higher-specificity override exists
FL+Chase complaint template (specificity 2)
Chase global fee auth applies to title search too
FL+Chase judgment timeline — Chase pushes harder (specificity 2)

name: Three-dimension specificity — Florida Chase FHA
description: Tests that specificity-3 overrides (state+client+investor) beat specificity-2 overrides.

expectedResolutions.explanation: FL+Chase+FHA (specificity 3) beats FL+Chase ovr-020 (specificity 2). 7-day deadline.
FL+Chase+FHA reduced fee (specificity 3) beats FL+Chase ovr-053 (specificity 2)
FL+Chase+FHA docs (specificity 3) — adds HUD face sheet and FHA servicing history on top of FL requirements
FHA-specific template for Chase in FL (specificity 3)

name: Four-dimension specificity — Florida Chase FannieMae Judicial
description: Tests the highest specificity level (4 dimensions pinned). Only templateId has a specificity-4 override.

expectedResolutions.explanation: Specificity 4 — all four dimensions pinned. Most specific override in the entire dataset.
No FannieMae-specific SLA override exists — falls back to FL+Chase (specificity 2)
No FannieMae docs override — falls back to FL state (specificity 1). FHA overrides don't apply to FannieMae.

name: Non-judicial case type — Texas
description: Tests caseType dimension overrides. Non-judicial cases have fundamentally different document requirements and skip the judgment step.

expectedResolutions.explanation: TX+NonJudicial (specificity 2) beats TX-only ovr-005 (specificity 1)
TX+NonJudicial (specificity 2) — completely different docs for non-judicial service
TX state 60-day service window (specificity 1)
Non-judicial cases skip judgment — SLA is 0 (specificity 1)
No fee for skipped judgment step (specificity 1)

name: New York complexity — NY Chase
description: Tests NY's heavier requirements (more filings, higher fees, longer timelines) combined with Chase's fee auth policy.

expectedResolutions.explanation: NY state 30-day deadline (specificity 1) — no NY+Chase override for this trait
NY requires 3 additional filings beyond default (specificity 1)
NY+Chase negotiated lower title search fee (specificity 2) beats NY-only ovr-012 (specificity 1)
NY judgment 360-day timeline — court backlog (specificity 1)
NY service fees higher (specificity 1)

name: Ohio with Wells Fargo — cross-dimension
description: Tests that a client-level override (Wells Fargo template, specificity 1) applies even in a state with its own overrides.

expectedResolutions.explanation: OH title search 21-day deadline (specificity 1)
Wells Fargo global title template (specificity 1) — different dimension than the OH SLA override, no conflict
OH requires extra affidavits for judgment (specificity 1)
Wells Fargo has no fee auth requirement — only Chase does

name: Illinois Wells Fargo FannieMae — state+client specificity
description: Tests IL+WellsFargo specificity-2 override beating IL-only specificity-1 where both exist.

expectedResolutions.explanation: IL+WellsFargo (specificity 2) — 15-day deadline
IL requires municipal lien search (specificity 1)
WellsFargo global title template (specificity 1)
IL 90-day service window (specificity 1)

name: Mostly defaults — Illinois Nationstar
description: Tests that when no overrides match for most traits, defaults are returned correctly. Illinois has a few state overrides but Nationstar has almost none.

expectedResolutions.explanation: No IL-only override for file-complaint.slaHours. IL+WellsFargo override (ovr-051) doesn't match Nationstar. Falls to default.
No title-search SLA override for IL or Nationstar. Default 30 days.
IL state override still applies — it doesn't require a specific client (specificity 1)
Nationstar has no fee auth policy — only Chase does
IL 90-day service window still applies (specificity 1)

name: Investor-level override tiebreaker — Florida VA
description: Tests effectiveDate tiebreaking when a state override and investor override have the same specificity. VA investor override (2025-03-01) should beat FL state override (2025-01-01) for requiredDocuments.

expectedResolutions.explanation: FL+WellsFargo (specificity 2) — 14-day deadline
VA investor override (specificity 1, effectiveDate 2025-03-01) beats FL state override ovr-002 (specificity 1, effectiveDate 2025-01-01). Same specificity — more recent date wins.
FL+WellsFargo service template (specificity 2)
FL 90-day service window (specificity 1)

name: Effective date filtering — FL Chase FHA as of 2025-07-01
description: Tests the asOfDate parameter. The FL+Chase+FHA overrides (specificity 3) have effectiveDate 2025-09-01, so they should NOT apply when resolving as of 2025-07-01. The system should fall back to FL+Chase overrides (specificity 2, effective 2025-06-01).

expectedResolutions.explanation: ovr-034 (FL+Chase+FHA, specificity 3) has effectiveDate 2025-09-01 — not yet effective as of 2025-07-01. Falls to ovr-020 (FL+Chase, specificity 2, effective 2025-06-01).
ovr-035 (FL+Chase+FHA feeAmount) not yet effective. Falls to ovr-053 (FL+Chase, specificity 2).

ovr-036 (FL+Chase+FHA docs, specificity 3) not yet effective as of 2025-07-01. Falls to specificity-1 candidates: ovr-038 (FHA investor, effective 2025-03-01) and ovr-002 (FL state, effective 2025-01-01). Same specificity — ovr-038 wins on more recent effectiveDate.
ovr-037 (FL+Chase+FHA template) not yet effective. Falls to ovr-025 (FL+Chase, specificity 2, effective 2025-06-01).

name: Ohio Chase FHA — cross-state investor override
description: Tests that investor-level overrides (FHA) apply in states that have no state+investor override. Also tests Chase fee auth stacking across all steps.

expectedResolutions.explanation: OH state title search deadline (specificity 1)
Chase global fee auth (specificity 1)
FHA investor override (specificity 1, effective 2025-03-01) — no OH-specific docs override for file-complaint exists, so this is the only match
OH state judgment docs override (specificity 1)
Chase fee auth applies to filing in OH (specificity 1)
